title: Moving Beyond Regulationism: A Response to David Norman
author: Rachel Burkey
orientation: Rachel Burkey systematically rebuts Dr. David Norman's two stated reasons for moving to indefinitely postpone the 2021 SBC Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, arguing that prior pro-life SBC resolutions were themselves regulationist compromises that legitimized child sacrifice rather than abolishing it. The piece contends that gradualism is not a stepping stone toward abolition but the principal stay of the abortion holocaust.
audiences: Pro-life Southern Baptist messengers and pastors
Abolitionist activists engaged in convention politics
Skeptics who view abolitionists as divisive
Historically minded readers comparing abortion to chattel slavery

questions.question: What specific parliamentary action did Dr. David Norman take against the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion at the 2021 SBC, and what was its intended effect?
questions.audience: Pro-life Southern Baptist messengers and pastors
questions.answer: Norman moved to lay the resolution on the table indefinitely, a procedural maneuver that, if successful, would have killed it without a substantive vote.
questions.quote: During the floor debate at the 2021 Southern Baptist Convention, Dr. David Norman made a motion to indefinitely postpone the [Resolution on Abolishing Abortion](https://southernbaptistsabolishingabortion.com/) — effectively killing it.

questions.question: Which three positions does the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion explicitly reject, and why does this place it at odds with prior SBC resolutions?
questions.audience: Pro-life Southern Baptist messengers and pastors
questions.answer: The resolution rejects any position that allows exceptions to legal protection of preborn neighbors, compromises God's standard of justice, or promotes God-hating partiality. Burkey argues earlier SBC resolutions did all three by endorsing restrictions, defunding-only stances, and partial-birth bans rather than total abolition.
questions.quote:
1) "****Allows for any exceptions to the legal protection of our preborn neighbors****,"

*2) "****Compromises God's holy standard of justice****," or*

*3)* ***"Promotes any God-hating partiality*** *(Psa 94:6; Isa 10:1-2; Prov 24:11; Psa 82:1-4)"*

questions.question: How does Burkey use the 1989 SBC resolution to expose the regulationist character of prior pro-life witness?
questions.answer: She points out the 1989 resolution merely called on legislatures to restrict abortion, equating that posture with the pro-abortion movement's old slogan of keeping abortion 'safe, legal, and rare,' and concludes the resolution should be repudiated as a failed Christian witness.
questions.quote: There is hardly a difference, if any at all, between this demand and the former slogan of the pro-abortion movement calling for abortion to be "safe, legal, and rare." It should go without saying among Bible-believing Christians that this resolution failed to be a consistently Christian witness to the culture. It should be repudiated.

questions.question: What inconsistency does Burkey identify between Norman's praise of Resolution #3 (taxpayer defunding) and Resolution #8 (the Uyghur genocide)?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activists engaged in convention politics
questions.answer: Norman lauded Resolution #8 for demanding China immediately cease the Uyghur genocide and free its victims, yet treated abortion only as a funding question — applying a higher standard of justice to the Uyghur than to the preborn.
questions.quote: Imagine if someone proposed an amendment to gut Resolution #8, changing it to say that the SBC merely opposes the use of taxpayer dollars towards funding the Uyghur genocide. Such an amendment would have been recognized as indifferent towards the plight of the Uyghur people, and a complete failure to say what needs to be said by the Church regarding this atrocity. Why does Norman — and other pro-life leaders within the SBC — fail to apply the same standard for the preborn as they do for the Uyghur people?

questions.question: What does Burkey argue is the actual moral effect of resolutions that endorse partial-birth abortion bans or informed-consent laws?
questions.answer: Such resolutions, by their structure, set the rules for when, where, and how a child may be killed, thereby legitimizing child sacrifice in violation of God's standard of justice.
questions.quote: Each resolution promotes legislation which, in violation of God's standard of justice, legitimizes the practice of child sacrifice by merely setting the guidelines of when, where, and how you can murder your baby.

questions.question: How does Elizabeth Heyrick's 1824 pamphlet help frame the article's critique of gradualism?
questions.audience: Historically minded readers comparing abortion to chattel slavery
questions.answer: Heyrick observed that slaveholders welcomed gradual emancipation precisely because contemplating it bred indifference to emancipation itself; Burkey applies the same dynamic to gradualist pro-life resolutions on abortion.
questions.quote: "[The slave holder] knew very well, that ****the contemplation of a gradual emancipation, would beget a gradual indifference to emancipation itself.****"

questions.question: How often have prior SBC resolutions actually called abortion 'murder,' and what does Burkey infer from that record?
questions.audience: Pro-life Southern Baptist messengers and pastors
questions.answer: From 1971 to 2015 the word 'murder' appeared only twice — once inside a 1974 pro-abortion resolution that branded the opposing-all-abortion view as 'extreme.' She concludes the SBC's pro-life record displays complacency toward the greatest genocide in human history.
questions.quote: Only twice was abortion referred to as "murder", and in one of those two instances, it was in a [1974](https://www.sbc.net/resource-library/resolutions/resolution-on-abortion-and-sanctity-of-human-life/) pro-abortion resolution that called opposing all abortion as murder an "[extreme](https://www.sbc.net/resource-library/resolutions/resolution-on-abortion-and-sanctity-of-human-life/)" position.

questions.question: How does Pastor Bill Ascol respond on the convention floor to the charge that abolition 'walks back' prior pro-life work?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activists engaged in convention politics
questions.answer: Ascol counters that the resolution walks nothing back; rather, it walks the convention forward toward immediate abolition without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: "This resolution doesn't walk anything back, it walks us forward towards the immediate abolition of abortion without exception or compromise."

questions.question: What contradiction does Burkey expose in Norman's celebration of Texas's SB8 Heartbeat Act and HB1280 Trigger Bill the weekend after the SBC?
questions.audience: Skeptics who view abolitionists as divisive
questions.answer: After opposing the abolition resolution, Norman thanked the Texas legislature for bills that, in Burkey's view, enshrine God-hating partiality based on arbitrary fetal capabilities and reinforce an idolatrous view of the courts — with SB8 actually being worse than the prior Texas status quo.
questions.quote: So not only did Norman stand against the abolition of abortion resolution, but he turned around and affirmed a bill that promotes God-hating partiality against babies with certain [arbitrary capabilities](https://freethestates.org/2020/07/tennessee-fetal-heartbeat-bill-a-step-in-the-wrong-direction/index.html), and a bill that strengthens an [idolatrous](https://freethestates.org/nullification-and-interposition/index.html) view of the courts.

questions.question: How does Burkey handle the question of Norman's personal sincerity while still indicting his position?
questions.audience: Skeptics who view abolitionists as divisive
questions.answer: She concedes Norman is likely well-intentioned and genuinely hates abortion, citing Article 10 of the resolution which acknowledges incremental laws are well-intended even though they sanction abortion, but insists pro-lifers must repent when shown their laws delay justice.
questions.quote: Article 10 of the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion said that though incremental pro-life laws effectively sanction abortion, they are well-intended. This is the case with many pro-lifers. But those pro-lifers have an obligation to shift their position when it is made clear to them that the laws they support are [unjust](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t1zMoSxevkg) and are actually [delaying justice](https://freethestates.org/2019/08/peeling-back-the-texas-pro-life-facade/index.html).

questions.question: How does Burkey deploy William Wilberforce against present-day gradualists like Norman?
questions.audience: Historically minded readers comparing abortion to chattel slavery
questions.answer: She quotes Wilberforce charging that the gradual abolitionists were the real maintainers of the slave trade — even while sincerely denouncing it — and applies that judgment to pro-life gradualists who, however sincere, prop up the abortion holocaust.
questions.quote: "Let me be forgiven if I speak strongly, where I feel so very deeply. ***It is not only because the gradual Abolitionists have been, in fact, the only real stay of that system of wickedness and cruelty which we wish to abolish;*** *though that assertion is unquestionably true;* ***but it is trying beyond expression that they should be the real maintainers of the Slave Trade, who reprobate it in terms of detestation as strong as any which we ourselves can utter.***"

questions.question: What pastoral hope does Burkey express toward Norman and other gradualists in her closing line?
questions.audience: Pro-life Southern Baptist messengers and pastors
questions.answer: She prays that brothers like Norman will recognize how their opposition to immediate abolition sustains the holocaust they oppose and that, upon recognition, they will repent immediately — without exception or compromise.
questions.quote: I hope and pray for my brothers like Norman, that they will see how the practical effect of their opposition to immediate abolition and insistence on gradualism is maintaining the abortion holocaust we seek to abolish, and upon this recognition will repent immediately – without exception or compromise.

related.title: Abolitionists Pass Resolution*, Make Huge Strides at SBC Convention
related.reason: Reports the floor outcome of the very 2021 SBC vote that this article responds to, providing the immediate event context.

related.title: Southern Baptist Convention Moves One Step Closer to Abolitionism (And Answering Objections)
related.reason: Companion piece on the same 2021 convention dynamics, including objections raised against the abolition resolution.

related.title: The Rise and Progress of Abortion Abolitionism in the Southern Baptist Convention
related.reason: Provides the longer historical arc of abolitionist organizing inside the SBC that culminated in the resolution Norman tried to kill.

related.title: SBC Pastors Publish "Resolution on Abolishing Abortion" for 2021 Convention Meeting
related.reason: Introduces the actual text and aims of the resolution Burkey is defending against Norman's motion.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Distills the core ideological contrast that drives Burkey's critique of regulationist SBC resolutions.

related.title: The Holiness of God Destroys Pro-Life Incrementalism
related.reason: Develops the theological basis Burkey gestures at when she calls regulationist resolutions violations of God's standard of justice.

related.title: Prominent Southern Baptist Voice Calls For Total Abolition
related.reason: Profiles Bill Ascol — whose floor reply Burkey cites approvingly — and his abolitionist witness inside the SBC.
